answer:
In music theory, parallel fifths or octaves occur when two voices move in the same direction by the same interval of a perfect fifth or octave.  
Option A has [EB] (E to B, a perfect fifth) moving to [Dd] (D to D, an octave), which is not parallel fifths or octaves.  
Option B has [EB] (E to B, perfect fifth) moving to [Gd] (G to D, perfect fifth), both moving in similar motion by the same interval, so this is parallel fifths.  

\boxed{B}
